title: arenaria tetraquetra subsp. murcica
intro: Arenaria tetraquetra subsp. murcica, also known as the Spanish sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its rich content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Arenaria tetraquetra subsp. murcica have been associated with various health benefits, including wound healing, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or taken as a supplement to support overall health and well-being.
